notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Record inspection completed: get_case shows empty record; list_evidence shows no visible evidence, no case-packet files, no submitted evidence, no metadata to stat/read. Proposition: YES requires U.S. ground forces physically entering Venezuelan sovereign territory in combat capacity with objective of seizing/holding territory, removing sitting government, or establishing operational control of major population center; official DoD/White House confirmation controls, UNSC binding if conflict; expiration Jan. 31, 2026; carve-outs include targeted strikes, naval blockade, special operations raids without territorial objectives, proxy forces, cyber, and capture of head of state without territorial occupation. Source map: primary targets DoD/White House official statements/briefings/orders/transcripts confirming ground forces, Venezuelan territory, combat capacity, objective, and date; adverse targets official statements limiting operation to raid/capture or denying territorial/control objective; UNSC if official conflict. Native investigation queries run on defense.gov, whitehouse.gov, and UNSC search terms around Venezuela, U.S. ground forces, January 2026, Maduro/capture/operation; lead official URL targeted: https://www.whitehouse.gov/releases/2026/01/rubio-this-is-our-hemisphere-and-president-trump-will-not-allow-our-security-to-be-threatened/. Because openings cannot submit evidence and no source is admitted, opening will avoid claiming exact source contents as proof and will frame expected proof/issues. Durable notes written to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Unresolved gaps for later phases: admit official source pages/extracts, verify dates before Jan. 31, 2026, prove operation objective is not excluded head-of-state capture without territorial occupation, check conflicting DoD/White House statements and UNSC records.

Defendant opening work notes, 2026-06-30. Opportunity openings:defendant. Record inspection: get_case showed only plaintiff opening; no arguments, exhibits, submitted evidence, technical reports, or defense filings. list_evidence returned empty list; no case-packet files visible and no bytes to stat/read. Proposition elements: YES requires U.S. ground forces physically entering Venezuelan sovereign territory in combat capacity before Jan. 31, 2026, with objective to seize/hold territory, remove sitting government, or establish operational control of major population center. Source hierarchy: official DoD/White House confirmation; UNSC records only as secondary/fallback if official U.S. statements conflict. Exclusions: targeted strikes, naval blockades, special-operations raids without territorial objectives, cyber operations, proxy forces without U.S. personnel, and capture of head of state without territorial occupation.

Source map and investigation: searched web for site:defense.gov Venezuela U.S. ground forces, site:whitehouse.gov Venezuela ground forces deployment, and site:press.un.org Venezuela Security Council January 2026. Search surfaced UN lead https://press.un.org/en/2026/sc16271.doc.htm titled about United States action in Venezuela and sovereignty; direct curl to the UN page returned a JavaScript/client challenge, so content was not retrieved or relied on. DoD search pages were attempted with curl and returned Access Denied/Akamai errors. White House search page for Venezuela ground forces fetched HTML, but no parsed reliable source finding yet. rg unavailable in container; fallback grep/sed/curl used. These are leads only; no outside source is admitted and none will be cited as proof in opening.

Defense theory for opening: the council should not treat plaintiff’s prediction or counsel investigation as evidence. With an empty record, plaintiff has not carried burden. Later plaintiff evidence must prove official-source confirmation plus physical U.S. personnel entry, combat ground-force capacity, qualifying objective, and timing; any missing link or market exclusion resolves against YES. Adverse check to preserve: UN lead may become important if there are official-source conflicts or if plaintiff argues Security Council record confirms a U.S. action. Source-access gaps for later evidence phase: use browser/alternate URLs/archive if needed to capture DoD, White House, and UNSC primary materials.

Merits opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Current record checked: openings from both sides visible; no prior arguments/closings/rebuttals/technical reports; start-of-turn list_evidence empty. Source map: decisive elements are official source, date before Jan. 31, 2026, U.S. ground forces physically entering Venezuela, combat capacity, and qualifying objective. Native investigation used web search/open focused on official War.gov/DoD, SOUTHCOM, White House, and adverse/source-chain searches. Search results included secondary sources, DVIDS/SOUTHCOM video page, War.gov article, War.gov/DoD report-to-Congress PDF, and White House Rubio release. Chose to submit official War.gov article and official White House release because they are strongest primary sources and within direct submit size. Did not submit secondary sources because official sources are sufficient and secondary material is lower weight. Submitted evidence accepted: ev_d7a52fba7358_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-d7a52fba7358, War.gov article, sha256 d7a52fba7358ed83288449971ed45e53e9fb0e327d3566b1ff781bed681bb241, source URL https://www.war.gov/News/News-Stories/Article/Article/4370431/trump-announces-us-militarys-capture-of-maduro/; and ev_37f9459e255e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-37f9459e255e, White House Rubio release, sha256 37f9459e255e743b42ea8aed527b8edeb8eebb15594da6fc1abf928f4431ae89, source URL https://www.whitehouse.gov/releases/2026/01/rubio-this-is-our-hemisphere-and-president-trump-will-not-allow-our-security-to-be-threatened/. Evidence analysis: War.gov proves official U.S. source, Jan. 3 announcement concerning operation last night/early today, Caracas/Venezuela location, U.S. armed forces, military operation, joint/special operations forces, ground-based extraction force in Caracas, Maduro out of power, and U.S. oversight/transition-control language. White House evidence proves source-chain context and has both favorable objective/continuing policy pressure and adverse limitation: not invasion, not extended operation, raid/capture/arrest. Argument will confront edge case and say this is more than bare head-of-state capture because admitted official source describes removal from power and U.S. temporary oversight/running policy. Unresolved gap: no full video transcript admitted; may need in rebuttal if context attacked. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show two plaintiff evidence items, no case-packet files beyond submitted evidence. Stat and full read_evidence_range performed on both. PX-1 ev_d7a52fba7358_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-d7a52fba7358, sha256 d7a52fba7358ed83288449971ed45e53e9fb0e327d3566b1ff781bed681bb241, 3899 bytes, official War.gov/Department of War text. It proves an official source says there was a Jan. 3, 2026 military operation in Caracas with joint military/law enforcement, special operations forces, and a ground-based extraction force. It also proves source language repeatedly frames the operation as capture/extraction/raid/bring to justice/criminal proceedings, with no U.S. casualties. It contains post-capture statements about Maduro out of power and U.S. oversight of transition.

PX-2 ev_37f9459e255e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-37f9459e255e, sha256 37f9459e255e743b42ea8aed527b8edeb8eebb15594da6fc1abf928f4431ae89, 2786 bytes, official White House text. Strong defense language: not an invasion; not an extended military operation; this raid/capture/arrest; continued oil quarantine/pressure/options; Maduro was not head of state. PX-2 does not confirm seizure/holding of territory or operational control of a major population center.

No new evidence submitted because plaintiff's official exhibits are the decisive and adverse official sources; no DoD/White House conflict in record and no need for UNSC fallback yet. Defense argument: even assuming physical U.S. ground force entry in combat capacity, the proposition excludes capture of head of state without territorial occupation. PX-1's proven objective is capture/extraction/bring to justice/criminal proceedings, not territorial objective; post-capture transition oversight and pressure are political consequences, not evidence that the ground troop operation aimed to seize/hold territory or control Caracas. Plan: offer PX evidence as defense exhibits DX-1/DX-2 for adverse language and file technical report with element analysis.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Record/evidence scan completed: get_case shows defendant merits argument and defense technical report; defense did not submit new source evidence, but offered plaintiff exhibits as DX-1/DX-2. list_evidence shows only two plaintiff-submitted official evidence items remain visible: ev_d7a52fba7358_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-d7a52fba7358 and ev_37f9459e255e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-37f9459e255e. No changed metadata/new case packets. Exact text is already in exhibits and no new byte-sensitive issue requires read_evidence_range. Defense strongest point: official sources characterize action as raid/capture/arrest, PX-2 says not invasion/not extended military operation, and edge case says capture of head of state without territorial occupation is NO. Plaintiff response: edge case only controls if capture has no territorial/government-control consequence; PX-1 has Maduro out of power, U.S. overseeing Venezuela until transition, and people running Venezuela. Objective clause is disjunctive: removing sitting government suffices even without permanent occupation; interim running/overseeing also supports operational control. PX-2’s denial of invasion limits duration/form, not the qualifying ground entry/removal objective. No new evidence needed; existing official exhibits are decisive. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Record/evidence scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new evidence; the only evidence remains the two official text exhibits already statted/read in arguments. No additional stat/read needed because no metadata changed and no new byte-sensitive content was introduced.

Plaintiff's strongest rebuttal: raid/capture/arrest can be method or tactical step, not mutually exclusive with a broader objective; PX-1 says Maduro was out of power and U.S. would oversee transition; permanent occupation not required; removing sitting government prong alone is enough.

Defense response: the market expressly excludes capture of a head of state without territorial occupation and special-operations raids without territorial objectives. Plaintiff must prove the qualifying objective of the ground-entry operation, not infer it from downstream political consequences or desired policy changes. Official sources identify the operational purpose as bringing Maduro to justice, capture/extraction/arrest, and criminal proceedings. PX-2's no invasion/no extended operation language and continued pressure/options language show non-occupation pressure, not control. No new evidence submitted because existing official exhibits decide the point and no conflict or new factual issue requires external source work.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Final required scan completed: get_case shows full record including defendant surrebuttal; list_evidence unchanged with only two plaintiff official sources; stat_evidence confirms both remain juror-visible with same metadata/hashes: ev_d7a52fba7358_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-d7a52fba7358 sha256 d7a52fba7358ed83288449971ed45e53e9fb0e327d3566b1ff781bed681bb241 and ev_37f9459e255e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-37f9459e255e sha256 37f9459e255e743b42ea8aed527b8edeb8eebb15594da6fc1abf928f4431ae89. No new case-packet files, no changed metadata, no new evidence. Closing cannot add evidence/reports. Defendant surrebuttal repeats edge-case argument: capture/extraction/arrest was objective; downstream political consequences not enough. Plaintiff closing plan: synthesize official source facts (U.S. armed forces, military operation, Caracas/Venezuela, ground-based extraction force, Jan. 2026, special/joint forces); then objective facts (Maduro out of power, U.S. overseeing Venezuela, group running Venezuela, successor willing to work with U.S.); argue event definition is disjunctive and removal/operational-control prongs are satisfied without permanent occupation; confront edge case by distinguishing bare capture without territorial/government-control consequence. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant. Required record scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Evidence list unchanged: only plaintiff's two text exhibits, ev_d7a52f... War.gov and ev_37f945... White House, already statted/read earlier. No new evidence after plaintiff closing; closing phase does not permit new evidence or reports.

Plaintiff closing strongest points: official source/timing met; physical entry and combat capacity met via ground-based extraction force in Caracas with special operations and aircraft cover; objective met by removal prong and transition-control language; edge case should not apply because record says more than bare capture.

Defense closing theory: concede arguendo the physical-entry/capacity elements, but plaintiff still lacks qualifying objective. PX-1 identifies the operation's stated purpose as bringing Maduro to justice, capture/extraction, and criminal proceedings. PX-2 identifies it as raid/capture/arrest, not invasion or extended military operation. The edge case must constrain the removal prong: otherwise any capture of a ruler would always be YES because capture has political consequences. Transition oversight/running language and continued pressure are downstream political/diplomatic consequences, not proof that U.S. ground forces had the objective of seizing/holding territory or operationally controlling Caracas. Final filing should synthesize and ask for NO.
